title: inga stipularis
intro: Inga stipularis, also known as the "Jatobá Branco" or "White Jatobá," is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. Native to the Amazon rainforest, it has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Inga stipularis can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent, allowing for the extraction of its bioactive compounds.
